summary: The most common type of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma is B-cell lymphoma, which accounts for over 80% of all cases. Although there is no direct evidence regarding orbital involvement, its occurrence in head-and-neck regions—commonly including near-orbital spaces—is typical of B-cell disease behavior.
